topic: A musician hears their song being performed by someone else
raw_output:
[story 1]

The air in the cramped rehearsal space felt thick, suffocating. Leo slammed his guitar pick onto the worn amp, the sharp crack echoing in the sudden silence. He’d poured six months of sleepless nights, every aching chord and whispered lyric, into that melody. And now, hearing it—perfectly rendered, flawlessly executed by some stranger—felt like a physical assault. His jaw clenched so tightly his molars ached. He paced the small room, his boots scuffing the dusty floorboards, his gaze fixed on the empty space where the sound had originated. Every nerve ending was buzzing with a frantic, restless energy. He wanted to rip the speakers apart, to erase the memory of that performance, to somehow rewind time until the sound never existed.

[story 2]

"No. No, that can't be right." Sarah whispered, her voice tight, as she stared at the stage where the acoustic set was winding down. The notes were his. Every intricate, sorrowful progression, every syncopated rhythm—it was his fingerprint on the air. A hot flush crept up her neck, turning her skin prickly. She gripped the edge of the backstage curtain until her knuckles were white, her breath coming in short, shallow gasps. She started shaking her head, a jerky, violent motion, trying to physically shake the intrusion away. The sheer audacity of someone else owning her creation made her want to scream until her throat tore.

[story 3]

He stood by the window, watching the city lights blur through the rain-streaked glass. The bass line hit him, unmistakable, a deep, resonant throb that belonged solely to his composition. A sudden, sharp intake of breath hitched in his chest. His hands clenched into fists so tight his nails dug crescents into his palms. He turned around, his eyes blazing with a furious intensity that seemed too bright for the dim room. He walked over to the soundboard, his movements jerky and uncontrolled, his fingers hovering over the controls as if he could physically wrestle the music back from the other person. A low growl escaped his throat, a sound raw with profound, burning indignation.
